content.problemStatement:
Take the sides of a triangle as user inputs and find if the triangle is equilateral, isosceles, or scalene.

**Note:** 

Equilateral Triangle: If all three sides of the triangle are equal, it is an equilateral triangle.

Isosceles Triangle: If at least two sides of the triangle are equal, it is an isosceles triangle.

Scalene Triangle: If all three sides of the triangle are different, it is a scalene triangle.


Input Format

The only line of input will contain three space separated numbers - The sides of a triangle.


Output Format

Output on a single line:

**Equilateral**, if the triangle is equilateral.
**Isosceles**, if the triangle is isosceles.
**Scalene**, if the triangle is scalene.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated positive integers a, b and c – the lengths of the sides of a triangle.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one of the words: "Equilateral", "Isosceles" or "Scalene" according to the classification of the given sides.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First case: two sides equal (2 and 2) → Isosceles. Second case: all sides different → Scalene. Third case: all three sides equal → Equilateral.
First test: all sides are 1 → Equilateral. Second test: 5, 7, 9 are all distinct → Scalene.
Case 1: sides 10,10,20 → two equal → Isosceles. Case 2: all equal → Equilateral. Case 3: sides 8,9,8 → two equal → Isosceles. Case 4: all different → Scalene.

solution.approach: For each test case we only need to compare the three side lengths. If all three are equal the triangle is equilateral. Else if any two are equal it is isosceles. Otherwise all three are different and the triangle is scalene. This requires O(1) work per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.
